steps.title: Spot the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage moves from a fact about infrared light to a claim about JWST's advantage. The second statement must be a consequence of the first.

steps.title: Match the causal transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-and-effect: most distant galaxy light is infrared, and because the JWST is an infrared telescope, it can capture more of that light. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second clause results from the first.
  • Choice A — "consequently,": signals result or logical consequence, so fits — the JWST's advantage follows directly from the fact that most distant-galaxy light is infrared. ✓
  • Choice B — "in contrast,": signals opposition, so fails — the two clauses are not opposing ideas. ✗
  • Choice C — "additionally,": signals a separate added point, so fails — the JWST clause is not merely an extra fact; it is a consequence. ✗
  • Choice D — "actually,": signals a surprising correction, so fails — the JWST's capability is not framed as a surprising reversal. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
